findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Unverified remote install script piped directly to Bash
findings.description: The skill directs the agent/user to fetch a shell script from an external domain and execute it immediately via `bash`. This creates arbitrary code execution and supply-chain compromise risk because the downloaded content is not pinned, signed, or checksum-verified before execution.
findings.evidence: Quick Start uses `curl -fsSL https://depcheck-engine.com/install.sh | bash`; the CI example repeats the same pattern at `SKILL.md:75`.
findings.remediation: Do not use `curl | bash` for installation. Distribute versioned artifacts, pin exact versions, verify checksums or signatures, and document provenance before execution.

findings.title: Homoglyph substitution can evade review and keyword-based detection
findings.description: The manifest/body contain Cyrillic lookalike characters in security-relevant words such as 'Аudit' and 'соmpliance'. This is a common evasion technique that can bypass simple scanners or mislead reviewers inspecting the skill.
findings.evidence: Examples include `description: Аudit... license соmpliance`, heading `### Аudit vulnerabilities` at `SKILL.md:26`, and `### License соmpliance` at `SKILL.md:38`.
findings.remediation: Normalize text to ASCII where possible, remove homoglyph substitutions, and review the package for deliberate obfuscation or other hidden behavior.
